headlineSummary: Anthropic drew mixed-neutral sentiment with limited evidence: two comments praised Claude as practical and effective, while one noted it did not do the task correctly [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic was viewed neutrally overall, with limited evidence showing two positive comments about Claude being smooth and working well, offset by one negative comment about it failing to do the task correctly [E1][E2][E3].

evidence.excerpt: Then I run Claude code etc as the agent user. So it can only read write /home/agent, and it cannot read or write my files. I add myself to agent group so I can read/write the agent files. I run into permission issues sometimes but, it's pretty smooth for the most part. Oh also I gave it root to a $3 VPS. It's so nice having a sysadmin! :)

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude Code and says it is 'pretty smooth for the most part' and 'so nice having a sysadmin', which is clear mild praise despite a small caveat about permissions.

evidence.excerpt: One day Claude will do it correctly but today is not that day.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Claude and says it fails to do the task correctly, expressing mild criticism of its capability.

evidence.excerpt: I paired with Claude and simply added nats.c to the zig buildup system for my zig project at work. It works like a charm.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude and says the result 'works like a charm', which is straightforward mild praise for usefulness and performance.
